2606.09189.title: Pretrained, Frozen, Still Leaking: Auditing Cross-Encoder Attribute Transfer in EEG Foundation Models
2606.09189.paper_summary: EEG foundation-model releases are usually audited one endpoint at a time: raw-reconstruction, membership inference, identity linkage, or DP-SGD on the downstream head. We audit the same released embeddings under all four endpoints jointly, on BIOT, LaBraM, and EEGPT, and show that each single-endpoint audit clears releases that still leak spectral attributes. The decisive evidence is a cross-encoder transfer audit: a single ridge attribute decoder learned from one frozen encoder transfers, via a fitted linear bridge, to held-out-subject test splits of every other encoder, with subject-disjoint matched-control 95% CI lower bound at least 0.081 across all six BIOT/LaBraM/EEGPT directions. We prove a sufficient condition: two encoders sharing a nontrivial attribute-coordinate projector overlap beta admit a chained ridge bridge attacker with centered-gain lower bound sqrt(beta/(1+tau^2)) - eps_br - rho_0, and back-solve beta in [0.008, 0.198]. To turn the joint audit into a deployment-readable decision rule we introduce an audit-endpoint disagreement score (AEDS), prove sufficient conditions for its positivity, and bootstrap-calibrate it per cell; AEDS is positive in all eight matched-CI cells (BIOT/LaBraM/EEGPT on EEGMMI; LaBraM on Sleep-EDF, 54-channel LIMO, CHB-MIT pediatric scalp EEG) with p<0.001, while a head-level Carlini LiRA membership audit reaches AUC only 0.50-0.70. Standard defenses fail under audit: a Wiener-style noise-aware adaptive attacker, the LiRA audit, and DP-SGD at every utility-preserving epsilon in {4,8} leave the attribute channel essentially unchanged. The contribution is an audit framework that turns scattered single-endpoint defenses into a joint release decision, supported by a cross-encoder bridge theorem and adaptive-attacker, LiRA, and DP-SGD baselines; the audit licenses release-blocking, not raw-waveform exfiltration or held-out-subject identity recovery.
2606.09189.paper_summary_zh: EEG 基礎模型的釋出通常是一次針對一個端點進行審計：原始重建、成員推斷、身份鏈接或在下游頭部的 DP-SGD。我們在 BIOT、LaBraM 和 EEGPT 上對所有四個端點共同審計相同的釋出嵌入，並顯示每個單一端點的審計都清除了仍然洩漏光譜屬性的釋出。決定性的證據是一個交叉編碼器轉移審計：從一個凍結的編碼器學習的單一脊屬性解碼器，通過一個擬合的線性橋轉移到每個其他編碼器的持出主體測試拆分，且主體不重疊的匹配對照 95% CI 下限在所有六個 BIOT/LaBraM/EEGPT 方向上至少為 0.081。我們證明了一個充分條件：兩個編碼器共享一個非平凡的屬性坐標投影重疊 beta，允許一個鏈式脊橋攻擊者，其中心增益下限為 sqrt(beta/(1+tau^2)) - eps_br - rho_0，並在 [0.008, 0.198] 中反向求解 beta。為了將聯合審計轉化為可部署的決策規則，我們引入了一個審計端點分歧分數 (AEDS)，證明其正性的充分條件，並對每個單元進行自助校準；在所有八個匹配 CI 單元中 (BIOT/LaBraM/EEGPT 在 EEGMMI；LaBraM 在 Sleep-EDF、54 通道 LIMO、CHB-MIT 兒童頭皮 EEG) 中，AEDS 的 p<0.001，然而頭部級別的 Carlini LiRA 成員審計僅達到 AUC 0.50-0.70。標準防禦在審計下失敗：一個 Wiener 風格的噪聲感知自適應攻擊者、LiRA 審計，以及在 {4,8} 中每個保留效用的 epsilon 下的 DP-SGD，基本上使屬性通道保持不變。這項貢獻是一個審計框架，將分散的單一端點防禦轉化為聯合釋出決策，並得到交叉編碼器橋定理和自適應攻擊者、LiRA 和 DP-SGD 基準的支持；該審計授權釋放阻止，而不是原始波形外洩或持出主體身份恢復。
2606.09189.author: Jianwei Tai et.al.
